sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: SLB tops second-quarter forecasts as growth in production systems offsets Middle East weakness
feed.summary: SLB reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding Wall Street expectations in both earnings and revenue, driven by robust international activity and growth in its Production Systems and Digital businesses. Although Middle East disruptions impacted performance, these were offset by strong subsea activity and demand for production technologies. The company's data center business also experienced significant expansion, and SLB maintained solid cash flow and announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Newmont (NEM) Reports Strong Q2 Earnings, Revenue Surges 15% Yea
feed.summary: Newmont Corp (NEM) reported strong Q2 earnings, with revenue increasing 15% year-over-year to $6.12 billion and profit reaching $2.2 billion, exceeding analyst expectations. The company's GF Score™ is 85/100, indicating robust financial health and growth potential, despite "Significantly Overvalued" valuation and $3.1 million in insider selling activity.

feed.title: TD Cowen raises Union Pacific stock price target on strong Q2, merger progress
feed.summary: TD Cowen increased its price target for Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) to $325 from $282, maintaining a Buy rating, following the railroad operator's strong second-quarter performance and increased earnings guidance. The company also made progress in addressing shipper optionality and is expected to file further details with the Surface Transportation Board. This positive outlook is further supported by upward revisions in earnings estimates by 16 analysts and impressive gross profit margins of 56%.

feed.title: Align Unveils New Digital Dentistry Innovations
feed.summary: Align Technology has introduced new innovations within its Align Digital Platform, designed to enhance the visualization, planning, and treatment aspects of digital dentistry. These updates include improved patient communication tools, more precise treatment planning capabilities for Invisalign cases, and streamlined workflows for both orthodontic and restorative procedures. The goal is to provide doctors with greater confidence and predictability, enabling them to treat a wider range of cases and improve overall patient experience.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) Shares Gap Down - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) shares experienced a significant gap down, falling approximately 7.3% at market open. Despite this, Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a price target of $199.46. The company recently reported earnings per share above estimates, though revenue slightly missed, and it offers a quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share.

feed.title: Revvity Developing Automated Platform for Tuberculosis Testing
feed.summary: Revvity is developing the T-SPOT A201, an automated platform for high-volume latent tuberculosis testing, with a planned launch in the second half of 2027 pending regulatory approval. This system is designed for large clinical laboratories to manage high sample volumes efficiently, reducing hands-on time per sample. The platform aims to improve the accessibility and efficiency of T-SPOT.TB testing at scale.

feed.title: Textron to Post Q2 Earnings: What's in the Cards for the Stock?
feed.summary: Textron Inc. (TXT) is scheduled to release its second-quarter 2026 results on July 28. The company's Q2 revenues are estimated to be $3.82 billion, a 2.8% increase from the prior year, while earnings are projected to be $1.52 per share, a 1.9% decline. Factors like higher aircraft and aftermarket revenues, along with increased sales in the Bell and Textron Systems units, are expected to boost performance, though higher costs could impact the bottom line.

feed.title: TSLP ETF Profile: Dividends, Returns (BOATS:TSLP)
feed.summary: The TSLP ETF, managed by Kurv Investment, Inc., aims to generate monthly income and provide exposure to Tesla (TSLA) stock price returns, capped on potential gains, using a synthetic covered call strategy. The fund has AUM of $19.14M USD, a dividend yield of 38.13%, and an expense ratio of 1.00%. TSLP primarily invests in bonds, with its top holdings being United States Treasury Bills.

feed.title: Chip design complexity drives Synopsys co-design approach in physical AI
feed.summary: Synopsys is addressing the increasing complexity of chip design in the era of physical AI through co-design methodologies and AI-powered agentic workflows. The company's recent acquisition of Ansys enhances its ability to simulate thermal stress while concurrently developing software for un-fabricated hardware. Synopsys also aims to democratize advanced chip design by offering cloud-based tools through a startup program and is exploring quantum computing for future computational challenges.

feed.title: NetApp’s DataPelago Buy Targets The Next Big AI Storage Bottleneck
feed.summary: NetApp has acquired AI infrastructure startup DataPelago to enhance its storage portfolio with GPU and CPU-accelerated data processing capabilities. This acquisition aims to help customers more easily find and utilize data for AI workloads, addressing a critical bottleneck in AI storage. DataPelago's technology, particularly its DataPelago Accelerator for Spark, is designed to significantly improve Apache Spark performance and reduce costs for qualifying workloads without requiring application rewrites or data migration.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: State Street + NYSE to Celebrate 'Fearless Girl'
feed.summary: The NYSE is providing a pre-market update, highlighting escalating tensions in the Middle East affecting oil prices and Vertical Aerospace's recent achievements. Additionally, the NYSE and State Street are co-hosting a summer block party to celebrate 'Fearless Girl', with Opening and Closing Bell ceremonies also planned.

feed.title: Insider Activity Update: Ronald W Tysoe Executes Options Exercise, Resulting In $889K At Cintas
feed.summary: Ronald W Tysoe, a Director at Cintas (NASDAQ: CTAS), exercised stock options for 5,048 shares valued at $889,710 on July 23, 2026. This transaction comes as Cintas shows positive financial trends with a 2.24% revenue growth and a strong gross margin of 50.95%, despite a higher debt-to-equity ratio compared to industry averages. Insider transactions, while not the sole investment factor, provide insights into a company's prospects.

feed.title: Ken’s Foods Partners with Rockwell Automation to Optimize End-of-Line Operations
feed.summary: Ken's Foods has partnered with Rockwell Automation to modernize its end-of-line operations, aiming to improve performance, reduce complexity, and support continued growth. By implementing on-machine architecture with Rockwell Automation PowerFlex 350 VFDs, the company achieved increased visibility, simplified maintenance, and reduced complexity, leading to an estimated 17% overall cost savings. This successful integration has established on-machine architecture as the standard for Ken's Foods' future pallet-conveying systems due to its efficiency and flexibility.

feed.title: TVA signs Ratepayer Protection Pledge to strengthen affordability rates for customers
feed.summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) has signed the Trump administration’s Ratepayer Protection Pledge, reinforcing its commitment to providing affordable energy. Interim President and CEO Mike Skaggs stated that TVA aims to maintain low energy costs and ensure data center expenses are not passed on to customers, especially as data center load is expected to double by 2030. TVA is currently exploring updates to its rate structure for large loads to support AI and other industry growth while balancing impacts on all customers.

feed.title: Halliburton Co Q2 2026: Revenue $5.714B, EPS $0.64— 10-Q Summary
feed.summary: Halliburton Co (HAL) reported strong second-quarter 2026 results, with revenue reaching $5.714 billion and diluted EPS at $0.64, both showing improvement over the previous year. The company attributed this growth to robust performance in drilling-related businesses and international markets, despite a decline in the Middle East/Asia due to regional conflict. Halliburton also noted strong demand for various drilling and completion tools across multiple regions.

feed.title: Smith & Wesson Adds to M&P15 Line with AXE Pistol & SBR in 300 BLK
feed.summary: Smith & Wesson has expanded its M&P15 line by introducing both a pistol and a short-barreled rifle (SBR) chambered in .300 Blackout. The M&P15 AXE Pistol, designed with suppressed shooting in mind, features Gemtech's GVAC direct impingement gas system to reduce gas exposure. While the pistol has an MSRP of $1,479, the SBR version's price has not yet been publicly released.

feed.title: Report: The Shifting Cruise Trends for 2026
feed.summary: A new survey from Cruiseline.com and Shipmate reveals strong demand for cruise vacations in 2026 and beyond, with 92.8% of respondents having at least one voyage booked. The study highlights evolving traveler preferences, including Royal Caribbean International becoming the top-booked cruise line and increased interest in destinations like the Mediterranean and Transatlantic voyages. Travelers are also considering longer itineraries and new cruise lines such as Margaritaville at Sea.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Universal Insurance tops Q2 2026 forecasts, shares jump
feed.summary: Universal Insurance Holdings exceeded Q2 2026 forecasts, with adjusted earnings of $1.84 per share and revenue of $621.3 million, leading to a 10.43% jump in stock price. The Florida-focused insurer attributed its strong performance to improved underwriting, a lower loss ratio, and favorable claims trends. Management highlighted a 33.2% annualized adjusted return on common equity and expressed confidence in continued profitable growth due to Florida's legislative reforms and rate-adequate business strategies.

feed.title: Digital Realty raises 2026 outlook after record second-quarter Core FFO
feed.summary: Digital Realty (NYSE: DLR) raised its 2026 Core FFO outlook after a strong second quarter, with revenue increasing 29% year over year to $1.92 billion and Core FFO reaching a record $2.65 per diluted share. The company reported a significant backlog of $1.9 billion and expanded its portfolio with fully leased data centers in Northern Virginia and a major land acquisition near Kansas City for future development. These achievements have led to an increased full-year Core FFO outlook, excluding net promote income, to $8.15 to $8.20 per diluted share.

feed.title: J&J, Gilead among recipients of EU drug nods this week (JNJ)
feed.summary: The European Medicines Agency's expert panel endorsed 12 new medicines and eight label expansions this week, impacting major pharmaceutical companies. Johnson & Johnson and Gilead are among the U.S. drugmakers receiving positive recommendations for their products. These approvals could allow J&J to enter the EU plaque psoriasis market and Gilead to secure first-line approval for Trodelvy in triple-negative breast cancer.

feed.title: NextEra Energy beats Q2 EPS estimates, Reaffirms 2026 Guidance - NextEra Energy (NYSE:NEE)
feed.summary: NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) reported stronger-than-expected Q2 adjusted EPS of $1.15, a 9.5% increase year-over-year, while revenue of $7.53 billion missed estimates. The company reaffirmed its FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance and highlighted significant growth in its renewable energy backlog, reaching 35.1 GW. NextEra also provided updates on its proposed merger with Dominion Energy, expecting the acquisition to close within 12 to 18 months, which aims to strengthen energy infrastructure and achieve substantial capital and EPS growth.

feed.title: BlackRock Is (NOT) Wall Street's Designated Adult - As BDEBT Highlights The Problem & The Hypocrisy
feed.summary: This article criticizes BlackRock's Private Credit Fund (BDEBT), arguing that it is unsuitable for individual investors despite its availability to them. The author highlights concerns about the fund's illiquid nature, the complex structure of its borrowers (often acquisition vehicles rather than operational companies), and the recent prorogation of investor withdrawal requests. The piece questions BlackRock's role as a "designated adult" in the financial industry given these issues, particularly when retail investors with moderate incomes are involved.

feed.title: Amgen hands in data package in hopes of FDA hearing for Tavneos defense
feed.summary: Amgen has submitted a data package to the FDA to defend the approval of its ANCA-associated vasculitis drug, Tavneos, which it acquired from ChemoCentryx. The drug faces regulatory scrutiny due to safety flags and concerns about its pivotal trial. Amgen is seeking a hearing after the FDA requested it voluntarily withdraw Tavneos from the U.S. market, arguing that the drug's benefit-risk profile remains strong, especially for the rare disease it treats.

feed.title: Google Goes Cash Flow Negative for First Time Since 2004, Is Apple the Anti-CapEx AI Trade?
feed.summary: Alphabet (Google) recently reported negative cash flow for the first time since its 2004 IPO, significantly impacting its stock due to high capital expenditures on AI infrastructure. In contrast, Apple has adopted a capital-light AI strategy, leveraging existing devices and outsourcing model development, which allows it to return more cash to shareholders through buybacks. This divergence in strategy positions Apple as a potential "anti-CapEx AI trade" amidst the high spending by hyperscalers like Google and Nvidia.

feed.title: HCA Healthcare (HCA) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare reported an 8.7% year-over-year revenue increase to $20.230 billion for Q2 2026, with diluted EPS up 11.6% to $7.62. Despite a negative payer mix shift impacting pre-tax income, the company revised its full-year 2026 guidance, expecting revenues between $77.0–$79.5 billion and diluted EPS between $28.70-$30.50. The outlook reflects ongoing adjustments for payer mix and Medicaid supplemental payment programs.

feed.title: Sensient (SXT) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Sensient (SXT) announced robust Q2 2026 results, with revenue growing 11.6% to $462.1 million and adjusted diluted EPS rising 25.5% to $1.20, driven by strong natural color conversion and strategic initiatives. The company raised its full-year 2026 guidance, now expecting mid-to-high teens growth in adjusted EBITDA and EPS, and reaffirmed confidence in continued momentum. This strong performance was attributed to new sales wins, favorable pricing, and operating leverage across all segments.

feed.title: Cigna expands AI capabilities to intervene earlier in chronic care
feed.summary: Cigna Healthcare plans to expand its care management programs by using AI and predictive analytics to identify and intervene earlier in chronic conditions for 20% more members. This expansion focuses on conditions like cancer, heart disease, and high-risk pregnancy, aiming to improve member health outcomes and reduce medical costs by an estimated $200 million over three years. The programs will offer personalized engagement through various communication modes and connect members with clinicians earlier to prevent conditions from becoming more serious.

feed.title: INVESTOR DEADLINE: Intuit Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit - INTU
feed.summary: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP has announced a class action lawsuit against Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ: INTU) for alleged violations of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. Investors who purchased Intuit securities between August 22, 2025, and May 20, 2026, and suffered substantial losses, have until September 8, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff. The lawsuit claims Intuit overstated its competitive advantages and growth, particularly regarding its TurboTax business, leading to significant stock drops after a workforce reduction and weak Q3 2026 tax season revenue report.

feed.title: Is It All One Big AI Trade?
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan's Dana Harlap explores whether the current market strength is solely due to AI or if other sectors are contributing. The article argues that AI's impact is broad and diversified across various industries, not just limited to technology. It also highlights the continued importance of diversification in portfolio strategy, noting that even when the semiconductor sector faces headwinds, other market segments can still perform well.

feed.title: Viking Holdings Ltd (VIK) Dividends & Stock Splits: Historical Payouts and Event Timeline
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of Viking Holdings Ltd (VIK) stock information, focusing specifically on its dividend history. It states that Viking Holdings Ltd has distributed $0.00 in dividends over the past five years, indicating no recent dividend payouts or stock splits recorded for the company. The page also presents current stock performance data and company financials, alongside various sections which are currently empty for detailed dividend data.

feed.title: SkyWest Q2 profit falls on higher fuel costs
feed.summary: SkyWest's Q2 profit dropped due to higher fuel costs despite a 7% increase in revenue. The company approved a $250 million stock repurchase increase and plans to expand its E175 aircraft fleet, with 11 new E175s secured for American Airlines. Analysts currently rate SkyWest as a "buy," with a median 12-month price target 20.2% above its recent closing price.

feed.title: VanEck Semiconductor ETF Options Spot-On: On July 23rd, 220.06K Contracts Were Traded, With 2.34 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) saw significant options trading activity on July 23rd, with 220.06K contracts changing hands, 2.34 million remaining as open interest, and a put/call ratio of 0.69. This high trading volume indicates considerable investor attention and speculative interest in the semiconductor sector.

feed.title: Ventas Inc stock hits all-time high of 99.84 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Ventas Inc. stock reached an all-time high of $99.84, representing a 49.89% increase over the past year. While InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock may be overvalued, it shows strong returns and is a prominent player in the Health Care REITs industry. Recent developments include positive analyst ratings, increased FFO estimates, an updated ATM Sales Agreement, and a declared quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Launches a New Spectrophotometer Suite for Water, Food and Life Science Testing
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched its new GENESYS G5 series of visible and UV-visible spectrophotometers, featuring three configurations (Vis, UV-Vis, UV-Vis Pro) built around a common software platform. This suite is designed to help laboratories standardize workflows and incrementally expand analytical capabilities across various applications, including water testing, food and beverage quality control, and life science workflows. The G5 series aims to reduce setup time, minimize method-to-method variability, and support regulatory compliance, offering connectivity options and accessory support for enhanced automation and data integrity.

feed.title: Kenvue Inc (KVUE) Earnings Forecast: Future EPS & Revenue Growth Estimates
feed.summary: This article provides an earnings forecast for Kenvue Inc (KVUE), noting its current earnings forecast score of 6.14 within the Personal & Household Products & Services industry. Analysts have assigned an average price target of $19.00 with a "Hold" rating. The article also details expected revenue for the next quarter, past EPS performance, and future EPS expectations.

feed.title: GLOBE LIFE INC. SEC Filing (Jul 24, 2026)
feed.summary: This article reports on a Form 144 SEC filing by Globe Life Inc. (GL) on July 24, 2026. The filing indicates a proposed sale of 37,000 shares of common stock acquired through a stock option exercise, with an aggregate market value of $6,329,960. The transaction was handled by APEX CLEARING and is scheduled for July 24, 2026.

feed.title: Ford recalls more than half a million Broncos due to engine fire risk
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. is recalling over 565,000 Bronco and Bronco Raptor vehicles from model years 2021-2026 due to a wiring harness defect that increases the risk of engine fires. The problem stems from insufficient protection on the wiring harness, which can lead to short circuiting and potential fires in the engine compartment. Dealerships will install new covers over the wiring free of charge, and Ford is not aware of any related accidents or injuries.
feed.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
feed.source_domain: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

feed.title: SLB Stock Surges After Impressive Earnings, Revenue Beat
feed.summary: SLB (NYSE:SLB) stock surged 9.4% after reporting strong second-quarter earnings of 55 cents per share and $8.97 billion in revenue, with a 5% revenue increase year-over-year. The stock has gained 35% year-to-date and is seen bullishly by analysts. Despite some technical resistance from its 80-day moving average, the company's strong performance and analyst sentiment indicate continued investor confidence.

feed.title: F5 (FFIV) Expanded Insight For BIG IP, Is The Stock Trading At A Discount?
feed.summary: F5 (FFIV) has expanded its Insight for ADSP platform with new fleet management tools for BIG-IP, aiming to enhance security workflows amidst accelerating AI vulnerabilities. While F5's share price has seen strong momentum with recent AI-focused updates and a 28.01% 90-day return, valuation narratives are mixed, with some suggesting a slight undervaluation around $409 per share and others identifying a potential discount of 18.8% based on a DCF model, despite a P/E ratio higher than a fair estimate. Investors are encouraged to consider both benefits and risks before forming their own view on the stock.

feed.title: What Telix Pharmaceuticals (ASX:TLX)'s Upgraded US$1 Billion FY26 Outlook Means For Shareholders
feed.summary: Telix Pharmaceuticals has significantly upgraded its FY26 revenue outlook to over US$1 billion, following positive early-stage data for TLX591-Tx, the initiation of Phase 3 development for TLX250-Tx, and a collaboration with Regeneron. This upgrade reinforces Telix's theranostic business model, aiming to leverage imaging success into higher-value therapeutic income. Despite the positive news, the company still faces execution and regulatory risks associated with its clinical trials and an increasingly competitive PSMA imaging market.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Joins SoftBank’s $40 Billion AI Loan Deal
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase is participating in a significant $40 billion bridge loan for SoftBank, aimed at supporting its artificial intelligence investments, including potential involvement with an OpenAI IPO. This deal highlights JPMorgan's central role in large-scale technology financing and could impact its future deal flow and fee-related revenue in the AI sector. Investors are advised to consider the bank's capability to secure similar mandates, although recent insider selling is noted as a risk.

feed.title: ProShares S&P Technology Dividend Aristocrats E...
feed.summary: The ProShares S&P Technology Dividend Aristocrats ETF (TDV) is recommended as a strong buy for long-term tech investors seeking dividend growth. Its portfolio of 38 technology companies benefits from robust free cash flow, especially in semiconductors and hardware driven by AI infrastructure. The ETF offers a modest 1% yield but targets stable income growth and lower volatility within the tech sector.

feed.title: Sunoco LP stock hits all-time high at 76.46 USD
feed.summary: Sunoco LP (SUN) has reached an all-time high stock price of $76.46, reflecting a 42.52% increase over the past year and a 46.8% year

feed.title: HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) Stock Price Up 4.2% Following Strong Earnings
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) saw its stock price rise by 4.2% after reporting stronger-than-expected quarterly earnings. The company exceeded analyst estimates with earnings of $7.59 per share and revenue of $20.23 billion, an 8.7% increase year-over-year. Analysts currently hold a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for HCA Healthcare.

feed.title: Nevada regulators approve key steps in MGM, Caesars take-private deals
feed.summary: Nevada gaming regulators have approved key steps for the take-private transactions of MGM Resorts International and Caesars Entertainment. This approval allows both companies to raise capital and moves forward Caesars' $17.6 billion acquisition by Fertitta Entertainment, with licensing approved for Fertitta Entertainment's CFO and General Counsel. Questions remain regarding MGM's Macau operations and Osaka project under a potential Diller-led private ownership, while Caesars prepares for integrating Golden Nugget's operations and addresses past AML compliance issues.

feed.title: Welltower stock hits all-time high at 250.14 USD
feed.summary: Welltower Inc. (NYSE: WELL) stock achieved a new all-time high of $250.14, representing a 54.66% increase over the last year. This surge is supported by strong Q1 2026 earnings, where EPS and revenue exceeded expectations. The company also announced plans for a C$750 million bond sale and received optimistic analyst ratings from Mizuho and Barclays.

feed.title: These Analysts Raise Their Forecasts On RTX Following Better-Than-Expected Q2 Results
feed.summary: RTX Corp. reported strong second-quarter results, surpassing analyst estimates for both earnings and sales. Following this performance and a raised full-year financial outlook, two analysts, David Strauss of Wells Fargo and Charles Minervino of Susquehanna, increased their price targets for RTX stock.

feed.title: Citi Drops Rand Bet After South Africa's Surprise Rate Hold
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. has closed its bullish position on the South African rand against the euro after the South African Reserve Bank unexpectedly kept interest rates unchanged. This decision contradicted analysts' expectations and severely impacted Citi's underlying assumptions, resulting in a loss of 2.13% on a $22 million notional position. Citi now expects the rand to remain "fragile" due to rising energy costs and the central bank's actions potentially affecting investor confidence in government bonds.

feed.title: Strattec Security (STRT): Insider Buy Signals Undemanding Valuation
feed.summary: Riverwater Micro Opportunities Strategy has added Strattec Security Corporation (NASDAQ: STRT) to its portfolio, noting that new management is driving margin expansion and customer diversification. The fund views recent significant stock purchases by the CEO and CFO as a strong signal of undemanding valuation. Despite this, Strattec Security is not among the 40 most popular stocks among hedge funds, and the article suggests that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside potential with less downside risk.

feed.title: Travelers (TRV) insider William H. Heyman plans Rule 144 sale of 5,206 shares
feed.summary: Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV) insider William H. Heyman plans to sell 5,206 shares of common stock under Rule 144 through Fidelity Brokerage Services on or after July 24, 2026. These shares were acquired as restricted stock vesting on February 21, 2024, as compensation. The aggregate market value of the planned sale is $1,978,280.00, and Heyman has reported other Travelers stock sales in the past three months.
